channel.description: Shell and Environment Variables Current shell Where a program is executed. Sub-shell (child shell) created within a shell to run a program. two types of variables: local (or shell) and environment. local variable Private to the shell in which it is created. Only used by programs that are started in that shell. environment variable Inherited from the current shell to the sub-shell during the execution of a program Value stored in an environment variable is accessible to the program, as well as any sub-programs that it spawns during its lifecycle.
